title: Ericoides setiferum
taxonomy: Ericoides setiferum is a type of flowering plant, and it belongs to the family Iridaceae. This plant is commonly known as the 'Wild Iris' or 'Sea Iris', due to its striking appearance and ability to thrive in coastal environments. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in sandy or rocky areas. The plant is a perennial, meaning it survives from year to year, and is known for its attractive, delicate flowers. Ericoides setiferum is a popular choice among gardeners and horticulturists due to its low maintenance requirements and ability to adapt to different climates.
